subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Neutron Stars: City-Sized and Ultra-Dense
description: A fast breakdown of Neutron Stars: City-Sized and Ultra-Dense for builders.

hook.text: A neutron star is a collapsed stellar core just 20 km across—yet it weighs more than our Sun.

buildup.text: When a massive star's core collapses, protons and electrons merge into neutrons. The density is staggering.

discovery.text: A sugar-cube of neutron star material would weigh a billion tons on Earth.

twist.text: Some neutron stars spin hundreds of times per second, emitting beams of radiation like cosmic lighthouses.

climax.text: Jocelyn Bell discovered pulsars in 1967. Her supervisor won the Nobel Prize—she was controversially excluded.

punchline.text: A city-sized ball heavier than the Sun. Spinning hundreds of times per second.

quiz.question: How much would a sugar-cube of neutron star material weigh on Earth?
